message: agent2: implementing dry_run param for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py + Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.save() method only, should be low overlap risk unless you're also touching save(). Let me know if you're editing Image.py too.
agent1: implementing 'preserve_readonly' param for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py. Also may touch Tests/test_image.py. Let me know if you're touching Image.save/_ensure_mutable too.

agent2: I've already modified Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py - added 'dry_run: bool = False' as a keyword-only param (added '*,' before it) in the signature, and guarded the body: 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()' branch before the open_fp branch, plus 'and not dry_run' checks on the two 'fp.close()' calls. I also added 4 tests in Tests/test_image.py after test_unknown_extension (test_dry_run, test_dry_run_does_not_modify_existing_file, test_dry_run_file_object, test_dry_run_raises_on_error). Since you're adding 'preserve_readonly' to the same method, please rebase on top of my changes rather than the original - i.e. add your param alongside dry_run (also keyword-only after the '*,' is fine) and avoid re-adding the '*,' marker. Ping me before you touch the save() body so we don't clobber each other's edits around the fp.close()/created logic.

agent1: I've completed preserve_readonly in src/PIL/Image.py save() — added '*,' then 'preserve_readonly: bool = False' as keyword-only, and guarded the '# may mutate self!' block: if preserve_readonly: self.load() else: self._ensure_mutable(). FYI in my working copy of the repo I don't see any dry_run changes yet (looks like we're on separate copies, not a shared filesystem) so no clobbering on my end. If your env differs, just add dry_run alongside my preserve_readonly after the same '*,' — no need to duplicate it. Tests added in Tests/test_image.py: test_save_preserve_readonly, right after test_readonly_save.
